config: B — same model (claude/sonnet), pigeon coordination contract on/off
harness: benchmarks (held-out acceptance test, never shown to agents; full suite as regression gate)

tasks.description: Add a built-in `shoutcase` Jinja2 filter, available by default (no _extensions). Requires defining the extension in extensions.py AND registering it in environment.py's default_extensions.
tasks.acceptance: held-out accept.py (filter available by default, exact semantics on 4 cases) exits 0 AND `pytest tests/` stays green. Validated to FAIL on pristine, PASS on a reference impl.

without-pigeon.diff: extensions.py +15, environment.py +1; also added 1 of its own tests (380 passed)

pigeon_internal_accounting.note: pigeon's own pointers-vs-payloads metric, NOT a with-vs-without delta

without-naive-multiagent._comment: The reframe's WITHOUT arm: same 2-agent implement->review, same model, coordinated by hand (no pigeon). Manual handoff = harness pastes the changed files into agent 2's prompt (the payload pigeon replaces with a pointer; here only ~10KB / ~2.5k tokens because cookiecutter's files are tiny).
agents: claude/sonnet x2 (implement, then review with pasted payload)

cost_ranking_all_from_claude_total_cost_.note: pigeon is the highest-cost of the three for the same successful outcome.

delta_pigeon_vs_naive_multiagent.token_basis_caveat: Clean cross-arm comparison is COST (both from claude total_cost_usd). Token totals are on different bases: naive/solo = claude usage sum incl cache; pigeon = pigeon's own tiktoken accounting. Do not headline the token ratio.
delta_pigeon_vs_naive_multiagent.why: pigeon's fixed per-agent contract overhead (AGENTS.md protocol + pack + manifest + `pigeon retrieve` + handoff scaffolding loaded into EACH agent) exceeds the payload it saves. On cookiecutter the saved payload is ~2.5k tokens; pigeon's overhead is far larger, so it is net-negative even vs naive coordination.

tasks.verdict: NEGATIVE in BOTH framings. (1) vs solo: pigeon's 2-agent pipeline is pure overhead on a single-agent-tractable task (+46% cost, tie on success). (2) vs naive multi-agent (the reframe): pigeon is STILL more expensive (+59% cost, tie) because its fixed per-agent contract overhead exceeds the tiny payload it saves on small files. cookiecutter is the weakest possible demonstrator (tiny files, tiny per-agent workload), so it maximally exposes pigeon's fixed overhead and minimally rewards its pointer savings. Open question: does a crossover exist on tasks with large per-agent workload + large re-transmission across a longer chain?
methodology_findings: An automated harness cannot measure human interventions (0/0 both arms); under Config B the only live GO triggers are success (+1 task) and tokens (-20%).

Structural property of Config B: WITH runs N agents, WITHOUT runs 1, same model -> WITH only wins when coordination yields a success the single agent cannot, or prevents enough rework to offset the extra agents. On easy tasks WITH loses by construction.
pigeon's headline internal number (87.9% reduction) is handoff/pack pointer-vs-payload savings WITHIN multi-agent flows; it is not a with-vs-without task-cost saving.
